title: Decision: Force-link imports with feature guards prevent compile errors when features disabled
description: secretx/src/lib.rs uses '#[cfg(feature = "X")] use secretx_X as _;' to force the linker to include each backend crate (lines 51–78). The pattern uses explicit feature guards matching the corresponding public feature flag. When a backend feature is disabled, the use statement is not compiled, preventing 'use of undeclared crate' errors. Without feature guards, disabling a backend feature would cause a compilation failure. The 'as _' pattern signals the compiler that this import is intentional despite being unused, documenting the purpose clearly.
